meta.title: MBS Question Bank — User 3 — Stream Selector
meta.delivery: Adaptive aptitude games + culture-fair items + forced-choice personality + interest sims + validity scale.

bias_controls.telemetry: Per-item response latency, long-string & answer-change logging on every block.
bias_controls.attention_checks: Embedded attention-check + infrequency items; careless responders flagged.
bias_controls.validity_band: Each report carries a confidence band (high / interpret-with-caution).
bias_controls.scoring: Forced-choice scored with Thurstonian-IRT; behavioural/game scores weighted above self-report; methods fused by confidence, not naive mean.
bias_controls.social_desirability: SD scale; if high, lean on game scores & show caution band.

items.stem: I learn new languages or word patterns easily.
items.constructs_fed: GMI-LING: MI: Linguistic (w=1.0) | WAIS-VCI: Verbal Comprehension (w=1.0) | CHC-GC: Crystallised intelligence (Gc) (w=0.8) | THUR-V: Thurstone Verbal (w=1.0)

items.stem: Complete the analogy: Hand is to Glove as Foot is to ___. (Correct = Shoe / Sock)
items.constructs_fed: WAIS-VCI: Verbal Comprehension (w=1.0) | CHC-GC: Crystallised intelligence (Gc) (w=1.0)

items.stem: Vocabulary MCQ: Which word is closest in meaning to "Tenacious"?  (a) Flexible  (b) Persistent  (c) Careless  (d) Generous  [Correct = b]
items.constructs_fed: WAIS-VCI: Verbal Comprehension (w=1.0) | CHC-GC: Crystallised intelligence (w=1.0) | THUR-V: Verbal reasoning (w=1.0)

items.stem: Odd-one-out: Which word does NOT belong?  (a) Oak  (b) Pine  (c) Rose  (d) Maple  [Correct = c: Rose — not a tree]
items.constructs_fed: WAIS-VCI: Verbal Comprehension (w=0.8) | CHC-GC: Crystallised intelligence (w=0.8) | CHC-GF: Fluid reasoning (w=0.6)

items.stem: Shape analogy: Circle → Sphere; Square → ___  [Correct = Cube]
items.constructs_fed: WAIS-VCI: Verbal Comprehension (w=0.8) | CHC-GC: Crystallised intelligence (w=0.8) | STR-ANL: Analytical intelligence (w=0.6)

items.stem: Verbal analogy: Doctor : Hospital :: Teacher : ___  [Correct = School]
items.constructs_fed: WAIS-VCI: Verbal Comprehension (w=1.0) | CHC-GC: Crystallised intelligence (w=0.8) | THUR-V: Verbal reasoning (w=1.0)

items.stem: Analogy: Architect : Building :: Composer : ___ [Symphony / Music]
items.constructs_fed: I-VER: Verbal Reasoning (w=1.0) | CHC-GC: Crystallised (w=0.6)

items.stem: If 3 pencils cost ₹24, how much do 8 pencils cost? (₹64)
items.constructs_fed: I-NUM: Numerical Reasoning (w=1.0) | CHC-GQ: Quantitative (w=0.6)
I-NUM: Numerical Pattern (w=1.0) | CHC-GF: Fluid (w=0.6)

items.stem: I can picture how shapes will look when rotated or rearranged.
items.constructs_fed: GMI-SPA: MI: Spatial (w=1.0) | WAIS-PRI: Perceptual Reasoning (w=1.0) | THUR-S: Thurstone Spatial (w=1.0)

items.stem: Mental rotation: which of (a)(b)(c)(d) is shape X rotated 90°? [correct = b]
items.constructs_fed: I-MR: Mental Rotation (w=1.0) | THUR-SP: Spatial Ability (w=1.0)

blocks.items_note: Game/simulation module — items generated/rendered at runtime by the engine.

items.stem: A bat and a ball cost ₹110 in total. The bat costs ₹100 more than the ball. How much does the ball cost? (Correct = ₹5)
items.constructs_fed: CRT: Cognitive Reflection (w=1.0) | WAIS-PRI: Perceptual Reasoning (w=0.4)

items.stem: If 5 machines make 5 widgets in 5 minutes, how long would 100 machines take to make 100 widgets? (Correct = 5 minutes)
items.constructs_fed: CRT: Cognitive Reflection (w=1.0) | WAIS-PRI: Perceptual Reasoning (w=0.5)

items.stem: Syllogism: All Blops are Frims. Some Frims are Goos. Therefore:  (a) All Blops are Goos  (b) Some Blops may be Goos  (c) No Blops are Goos  (d) All Goos are Blops  [Correct = b]
items.constructs_fed: CHC-GF: Fluid reasoning (w=1.0) | STR-ANL: Analytical intelligence (w=1.0) | CRT: Cognitive Reflection (w=0.6)

items.stem: Number series: What comes next?  3, 6, 12, 24, ___  [Correct = 48]
items.constructs_fed: CHC-GF: Fluid reasoning (w=1.0) | WAIS-PRI: Perceptual Reasoning (w=0.8) | RAVEN: Raven matrices style (w=0.8)

items.stem: Arithmetic: A class of 30 splits into groups of 4. How many complete groups?  [Correct = 7]
items.constructs_fed: CHC-GF: Fluid reasoning (w=0.8) | WAIS-PRI: Perceptual Reasoning (w=0.8) | THUR-N: Thurstone Number (w=1.0)

items.stem: Percentage: A shirt costs ₹500 discounted 20%. Final price?  [Correct = ₹400]
items.constructs_fed: THUR-N: Thurstone Number (w=1.0) | CHC-GF: Fluid reasoning (w=0.6) | WAIS-PRI: Perceptual Reasoning (w=0.6)

items.stem: I enjoy building or repairing things with my hands.
items.constructs_fed: RIASEC-R: Realistic (w=1.0) | GMI-BOD: Bodily-Kinaesthetic (w=0.5)

items.stem: I'd rather work outdoors than in an office.
items.constructs_fed: RIASEC-R: Realistic (w=1.0) | WC-OUT: Work Context Outdoors (w=0.8)

items.stem: I like operating tools, vehicles or machinery.
items.constructs_fed: RIASEC-R: Realistic (w=1.0)

items.stem: I prefer practical, concrete problems over abstract ones.
items.constructs_fed: RIASEC-R: Realistic (w=1.0) | MBTI-SN: Sensing (w=0.4)

items.stem: I enjoy physical activity as part of my work.
items.constructs_fed: RIASEC-R: Realistic (w=1.0)

items.stem: Working with animals or in nature appeals to me.
items.constructs_fed: RIASEC-R: Realistic (w=1.0)

items.stem: I like investigating how things work scientifically.
items.constructs_fed: RIASEC-I: Investigative (w=1.0) | BIG5-O: Openness (Ideas) (w=0.6) | NFC: Need for Cognition (w=0.6)

items.stem: I enjoy solving complex puzzles or research questions.
items.constructs_fed: RIASEC-I: Investigative (w=1.0) | I-ANL: Analytical Intelligence (w=0.5)

items.stem: I'd like to work in a lab or with data.
items.constructs_fed: RIASEC-I: Investigative (w=1.0)

items.stem: I read articles about science, technology or medicine for fun.
items.constructs_fed: RIASEC-I: Investigative (w=1.0) | NFC: Need for Cognition (w=0.5)

items.stem: I prefer figuring out problems by thinking deeply rather than asking others.
items.constructs_fed: RIASEC-I: Investigative (w=1.0) | BIG5-E: Extraversion (w=0.3)

items.stem: I enjoy collecting and analysing information.
items.constructs_fed: RIASEC-I: Investigative (w=1.0) | BIG5-C: Conscientiousness (w=0.3)

items.stem: I see myself as someone who is talkative and outgoing.
items.constructs_fed: BIG5-E: Extraversion (w=1.0) | MBTI-EI: Extraversion vs Introversion (w=1.0) | DISC-I: Influence (w=0.8)

items.stem: I sympathise with others' feelings.
items.constructs_fed: BIG5-A: Agreeableness (w=1.0) | EQ-EM: Empathy (w=1.0) | DISC-S: Steadiness (w=0.6) | SS-COLLAB: Empathy & Care (w=1.0)

items.stem: People should pay attention to me — I am a natural leader.
items.constructs_fed: DT-N: Narcissism (w=1.0) | BIG5-E: Extraversion (w=0.4) | DISC-D: Dominance (w=0.6)

items.stem: I am usually the first to volunteer for new tasks or projects.
items.constructs_fed: BIG5-E: Extraversion (w=0.7) | BIG5-C: Conscientiousness (w=0.6) | DISC-D: Dominance (w=0.5) | GRIT-PE: Perseverance (w=0.4)

items.stem: Semantic diff — Social energy: Being around many people makes me feel:  [1 = Drained … 5 = Energised]
items.constructs_fed: BIG5-E: Extraversion (w=1.0) | MBTI-EI: Extraversion vs Introversion (w=0.8) | DISC-I: Influence (w=0.6)

items.stem: Forced-choice — Which is most like you?  (a) I love debate and argument  (b) I prefer to keep peace  (c) I decide on logic  (d) I decide on feelings
items.constructs_fed: MBTI-TF: Thinking vs Feeling — (c)/(d) | BIG5-A: Agreeableness — (b) | DISC-D: Dominance — (a)

items.stem: I work hard even when no one is watching.
items.constructs_fed: BIG5-C: Conscientiousness (Self-Discipline) (w=1.0) | GRIT-PE: Perseverance (w=0.6) | WST-ID: Integrity (w=0.5)

items.stem: I am usually the one who starts a conversation.
items.constructs_fed: BIG5-E: Extraversion (Assertiveness) (w=1.0) | DISC-D: Dominance (w=0.6) | L-INI: Leadership Initiation (w=0.5)

items.stem: I take charge in group situations.
items.constructs_fed: DISC-D: Dominance (w=1.0) | BIG5-E: Assertiveness (w=0.5) | L-INI: Initiation (w=0.6)

items.stem: I am comfortable with steady, predictable routines.
items.constructs_fed: DISC-S: Steadiness (w=1.0) | BIG5-C: Conscientiousness (Order) (w=0.4)

items.stem: I prefer following clear rules and procedures.
items.constructs_fed: DISC-C: Conscientiousness (w=1.0) | BIG5-C: Conscientiousness (w=0.6) | HOF-UA: Uncertainty Avoidance (w=0.5)

items.stem: I keep my promises to myself.
items.constructs_fed: BIG5-C: Self-Discipline (w=1.0) | WST-ID (w=0.6)

items.stem: Right now, I am mostly focused on basic safety and security in my life.
items.constructs_fed: MASLOW-1: Physiological/Safety (w=1.0) | W-FIN: Financial wellness (w=0.4)

items.stem: I feel close and connected to people who matter to me.
items.constructs_fed: MASLOW-3: Belonging (w=1.0) | SDT-REL: Relatedness (w=1.0) | W-SOC: Social wellness (w=1.0) | ATT-S: Attachment Security (w=0.8)

items.stem: I feel respected by the people around me.
items.constructs_fed: MASLOW-4: Esteem (w=1.0) | SDT-COM: Competence (w=0.6) | OB-PS: Psychological Safety (w=0.6)

items.stem: I feel I am living a life that expresses who I truly am.
items.constructs_fed: MASLOW-5: Self-Actualisation (w=1.0) | W-SPI: Spiritual wellness (w=1.0) | JCM-MM: Meaningful work (w=0.8)

items.stem: What energises me most is getting recognition and a sense of achievement.
items.constructs_fed: HZ-MOT: Herzberg Motivators (w=1.0) | MCC-N-ACH: Need for Achievement (w=1.0)

items.stem: I leave a job/role mostly because of poor conditions, pay or supervision — not because the work itself was unrewarding.
items.constructs_fed: HZ-HYG: Herzberg Hygiene factors (w=1.0) | EQT-S: Equity Sensitivity (w=0.5)

items.stem: Your friend has misunderstood something you said. You would most likely: (a) Wait for the issue to blow over; (b) Send a brief text explaining; (c) Meet face-to-face and listen first; (d) Get a mutual friend to mediate.
items.constructs_fed: SS-COMM: Communication competence (w=1.0) | SS-COLLAB: Relationship repair (w=0.7) | EQ-EM: Empathy (w=0.5)

items.stem: Your group project has one team-mate not contributing. You would most likely: (a) Cover their part silently; (b) Tell the teacher / manager; (c) Have a direct, kind conversation with them; (d) Confront them in front of the group.
items.constructs_fed: SS-COLLAB: Collaboration & conflict skill (w=1.0) | SS-COMM: Assertive communication (w=0.7) | L-TRANS: Coaching behaviour (w=0.3)

items.stem: I plan my day and stick to my plan most of the time.
items.constructs_fed: SS-SELF: Self-Management framework (w=1.0) | SELF-GS: Goal-Setting (w=1.0) | BIG5-C: Conscientiousness (w=0.7)

items.stem: I delay things I should be doing.
items.constructs_fed: SS-SELF: Self-Management framework (w=1.0) | SELF-SC: Self-Control (w=1.0) | BIG5-C: Conscientiousness (w=0.6)

items.stem: Age (number/slider — branches to school/college/workforce flow)
items.constructs_fed: DEMO-AGE: Age (w=1.0) | ROUTE: Branching (w=1.0)

items.stem: Gender
items.constructs_fed: DEMO-GEN: Gender (w=1.0)

items.stem: Current education level (determines subject list in Phase 11)
items.constructs_fed: DEMO-EDU: Education Level (w=1.0) | ROUTE: Branching (w=1.0)
